{"id":"project-LICHEN-pihp","title":"RplTarget prefix length not validated against maximum","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dao.py:37-54

The RplTarget class allows prefix_length up to 255 (uint8), but IPv6 addresses are only 128 bits. A prefix_length > 128 is invalid.

In to_option():
```python
nbytes = (self.prefix_length + 7) // 8  # Could be up to 32 bytes
data = bytes([0, self.prefix_length]) + self.target.packed[:nbytes]
```

If prefix_length=255, nbytes=32, but target.packed is only 16 bytes. The slice [:32] just returns all 16 bytes, which is semantically wrong - it should fail.

In from_option():
```python
prefix_length = opt.data[1]
nbytes = (prefix_length + 7) // 8
prefix = opt.data[2 : 2 + nbytes]
if len(prefix) != nbytes:
    raise DaoError("RPL Target prefix shorter than prefix length")
```

If prefix_length=255 in received data, nbytes=32, and if the option has 32 bytes of address data (malicious/malformed), it would be accepted but is semantically invalid.

Fix: Validate prefix_length <= 128 in both to_option() and from_option().","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:27.459452Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:27.459486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-pihp","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.9.4","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-07-18T20:28:04.25266Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-pihp","title":"RplTarget prefix length not validated against maximum","description":"File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dao.py:37-54

The RplTarget class allows prefix_length up to 255 (uint8), but IPv6 addresses are only 128 bits. A prefix_length > 128 is invalid.

In to_option():
```python
nbytes = (self.prefix_length + 7) // 8  # Could be up to 32 bytes
data = bytes([0, self.prefix_length]) + self.target.packed[:nbytes]
```

If prefix_length=255, nbytes=32, but target.packed is only 16 bytes. The slice [:32] just returns all 16 bytes, which is semantically wrong - it should fail.

In from_option():
```python
prefix_length = opt.data[1]
nbytes = (prefix_length + 7) // 8
prefix = opt.data[2 : 2 + nbytes]
if len(prefix) != nbytes:
    raise DaoError("RPL Target prefix shorter than prefix length")
```

If prefix_length=255 in received data, nbytes=32, and if the option has 32 bytes of address data (malicious/malformed), it would be accepted but is semantically invalid.

Fix: Validate prefix_length <= 128 in both to_option() and from_option().","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:27.459452Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T23:32:44.53857457Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T23:32:44.53857457Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T23:37:44.53857457Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T23:32:44.53857457Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-pihp","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-2auf.44.9.4","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-07-18T20:28:04.25266Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Added explicit prefix_length <=128 validation in RplTarget.to_option() and from_option() to match Rust impl and prevent invalid nbytes calculations or malformed packets. Also resolved unrelated merge conflicts in ipv6/packet.py, rpl/visualize.py, and lichen-rpl/message.rs to restore import/testability. ruff clean, manual validation tests pass, vectors/tests unaffected.
